iti24:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti24:2.1:
“Monks, if a single person were to wander & transmigrate on for an eon,
he/she would leave behind a chain of bones, a pile of bones,
a heap of bones, as large as this Mount Vepulla,
if there were someone to collect them and the collection were not destroyed.”
iti24:3.1: The accumulation of a single person’s
iti24:3.3: would be a heap on a par with the mountain,
iti24:4.3: to the north of Vulture Peak
iti24:4.4: in the mountain-ring of the Magadhans.)
iti24:5.1: But when that person sees with right discernment
iti24:5.6: the way to the stilling of stress–
iti24:6.1: having wandered on seven times at most, then,
iti24:6.3: with the ending of all fetters,
iti24:6.4: he makes an end of stress.
